event: Sino-French War: French destroy the Fujian fleet at Fuzhou
Treaty of Tientsin ends the Sino-French war
First Sino-Japanese War opens (Battle of Pungdo)
Battle of the Yalu: Beiyang fleet mauled
Port Arthur falls to Japan
Weihaiwei falls; Beiyang fleet annihilated
Treaty of Shimonoseki: 230m tael indemnity forces massive foreign borrowing
Anglo-German indemnity loan (5% 1896) issued
Germany seizes Kiaochow; scramble for concessions begins
4.5% Gold Loan 1898 (second Anglo-German indemnity loan) signed
Coup ends the Hundred Days' Reform; Cixi resumes control
Boxer Rising: siege of the Peking legations begins; war declared on the powers
Eight-Nation Alliance relieves Peking
Boxer Protocol: 450m tael indemnity secured on customs and salt revenue
Russo-Japanese War fought largely on Chinese soil
Deaths of the Guangxu Emperor and Empress Dowager Cixi; child emperor Puyi
Hukuang Railways loan signed; railway-rights agitation in Sichuan
Wuchang rising: the Xinhai Revolution begins
Abdication of Puyi: the Qing dynasty ends; Republic under Yuan Shikai
Reorganisation Loan (25m pounds) signed with the five-power consortium
Second Revolution: KMT provinces rise against Yuan (crushed by September)
Japan presents the Twenty-One Demands
Yuan Shikai dies; central authority dissolves into warlordism
China declares war on Germany; customs revenues strained
Zhili-Anhui war opens the warlord-war decade in the north
Defaults spread across unsecured Chinese sterling obligations
First Zhili-Fengtian war around Peking
Second Zhili-Fengtian war
May Thirtieth movement: anti-foreign strikes and boycotts
Northern Expedition launched from Canton
Nanking Incident: foreign consulates attacked; KMT-CCP split follows
Nationalists take Peking; nominal reunification under Nanking
Nanking begins rehabilitating foreign-debt service (customs autonomy)
